title: SEMANTIC FLIP -- let the B-164 semantic router ACT as a tie-breaker (safe read-only lanes only), behind M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP
worktree: ../../M8-b166 (off origin/main @ ec317ca = the merged B-164 shadow + B-165 wallet)
status: code complete; offline-verified (new PS mirror all-pass + 9/9 regression green incl. updated B-164); NOT deployed -- pending Muhammad's OK to (1) merge feat/b166-semantic-flip -> main and (2) set M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP=1 on Vercel prod. Flag is DEFAULT OFF, so merging is inert/safe; the flip only activates when the env flag is set. Prod /api/chat is the only real proof (PS mirrors can't run the JS or embed).
goal: On the turns where the DETERMINISTIC router is UNSURE, adopt the B-164 semantic pick -- but ONLY when it is confident+decisive AND lands in a SAFE read-only lane {knowledge, web, memory}. Headline win: 'tell me about my kafala operation' (and doc-topic questions like it) finally route to his INGESTED docs (cited KG path), not a generic memory/web answer.

threshold_decision.where_from: Set from the B-164 shadow dataset on BOLT Supabase ltqpoupferwituusxwal -> m8_router_misses, lane like 'arbiter:sem:%', 2026-06-29 -> 06-30 (the full shadow log since B-164 merged). Each arbiter:sem:<domain> row was paired (by redacted message + <=15s) to the SAME turn's REAL lane to sort true-fixes from false-positives.
threshold_decision.only_safe_lane_picks_can_be_adopted: Of all sem shadow picks, only domain in {knowledge, web, memory} is adoptable. Distribution: wallet 15, finance 12, chat 9, knowledge 3, docs 2, notes 2, tasks 1, fleet 1, memory 1, web 0. Everything except knowledge/web/memory is domain-excluded (cannot be flipped), which is ALSO what keeps the wallet/fleet/finance money-safety lanes on the B-152 arbiter.

safe_lane_evidence.TRUE_FIX: 'tell me about my kafala operation' -> sem:knowledge conf=0.94 margin=0.28 (logged twice, identical). REAL route was fleet@0.60 + lk:memory/reg:memory@0.70 -> served a generic memory/web answer. THIS is the one the flip must capture.
FALSE_POSITIVES_in_safe_lanes: 'so the ones at the top of that queue ... present those as our key findings. confirm' -> sem:knowledge conf=0.62 margin=0.01 (really a chat confirm).
'why did sara [spend] on a car in june?' -> sem:memory conf=0.69 margin=0.04 (really WALLET; arb routed it wallet@0.90 correctly).

threshold_decision.separation: True fix sits at conf 0.94 / margin 0.28; the MAX safe-lane false positive is conf 0.69 / margin 0.04. Huge gap. T=0.85 (FP max 0.69 + 0.16 cushion, below 0.94 by 0.09) and M=0.15 (FP max 0.04 + 0.11 cushion, below 0.28 by 0.13) cut cleanly between them. STRICT per 'data is thin -> a missed fix is fine, a wrong override is not'; not overfit to the lone 0.94 point. Highest-conf NON-safe pick (wallet 0.79) is excluded TWICE: by domain (not in safe set) AND by conf (<0.85).

what_flips.kafala_and_like: 'tell me about my kafala operation' (knowledge 0.94/0.28) -> ADOPT -> lookup={knowledge} -> forceKnowledgeLookup -> the existing cited knowledge-graph path (suppresses web + fleet, opens kgGate). No KG hit => soft fall-through (unchanged B-156/B-160 contract).
what_flips.nothing_else_in_the_shadow: With T=0.85/M=0.15 + safe-domain, the flip adopts EXACTLY 1 turn in the entire shadow dataset (kafala) and ZERO false positives. Every other shadow turn is unchanged.

critical_design_note_prod_flag_interacti: M8_REGISTRY_LOOKUP=1 is ALREADY live on prod (proven by the shadow: kafala logged lk:memory). So B-156 PRE-ATTACHES lookup={memory} for kafala BEFORE the flip runs. A naive `!lookup` guard would therefore BLOCK the flip and the kafala fix would NEVER fire on prod (classic 'mirror passes, live fails'). FIX: the flip overrides an EMPTY lookup OR the INERT telemetry-only `memory` attach (memory does nothing downstream -- orchestrator only logs it), but NEVER overrides an ACTING B-156 knowledge/web lookup. Guard = `(!lookup || lookup.domain === "memory")`.
files: lib/semantic-router.js (+FLIP_CONF/FLIP_MARGIN/FLIP_SAFE_DOMAINS consts + pure shouldFlip(sem) predicate + exports; scoreSemantic/EXEMPLARS/cosine UNCHANGED)

lib/orchestrator.js (resolveDomainRoute: import shouldFlip; inside the existing B-164 !_clearWin block, after the shadow log, the flip sets lookup via the B-156 channel behind M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP + logs a distinct 'sem-flip:' lane)

tests/build164_semantic_router.test.ps1 (UPDATED -- the 'shadow block does NOT reassign lookup' assertion now reads 'lookup reassigned ONLY behind M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP'; arb/crud 'never touched' checks kept)
not_touched: domain-arbiter.js (the B-152 wallet<->fleet money-safety decision `arb` is NEVER reassigned by the flip -- proven statically in the mirror)
the privacy wall (only message TEXT is embedded -- the B-164 contract; no money figure ever enters an embed or LLM prompt)
scoreSemantic / EXEMPLARS / cosine / embedExemplars (B-164 logic unchanged)
the B-156 downstream lookup consumers (forceKnowledgeLookup / web->LOOKUP) -- the flip REUSES the same already-tested channel, no new downstream code
No new api/*.js (Vercel 12-fn cap is full)

safety.money_lanes: wallet/fleet/finance are NOT in FLIP_SAFE_DOMAINS -> shouldFlip() returns false for them even at conf/margin 0.99 -> the route stays on the B-152 arbiter. The flip never reassigns `arb`.
safety.flag_off: M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP unset/!=1 => the new branch is dead => byte-for-byte B-164 (regression suite proves this).
safety.fail_safe: The flip lives inside the existing B-164 try/catch; any error => pre-flip behaviour. It also only runs inside the !_clearWin cost guard (det must be UNSURE) so a confident keyword turn never reaches it.
safety.no_override: Never overrides an ACTING B-156 knowledge/web lookup (only empty or inert memory).

tests.new_mirror: tests/build166_semantic_flip.test.ps1 (45 checks, all PASS): [1] parses FLIP_CONF/FLIP_MARGIN/FLIP_SAFE_DOMAINS from the JS and asserts 0.85/0.15/{knowledge,web,memory} (drift trips it); [2] shouldFlip() truth table on the REAL shadow rows (kafala 0.94/0.28 ADOPT; queue-confirm 0.62/0.01 NO; sara-car memory 0.69/0.04 NO; boundary 0.85/0.15 inclusive; money lanes NO even at 0.99/0.99); [3] FULL gate (flag OFF=no-op; det clear-win=no flip; B-156 pre-attached memory=FLIP override [the real prod case]; acting knowledge/web=NO override; money=NO); [4] static wire (flag-gated, inside !_clearWin + try/catch, assigns only lookup never arb/crud, why=semantic_flip, distinct sem-flip lane).
tests.regression: 9/9 GREEN (exit 0): build152_arbiter, build155_registry, build156_lookup, build157_walletfleet, build160_askmydocs, build163_routing, build164_semantic_router (updated), build165_wallet_range_breakdown, build166_semantic_flip(new). Baseline of all 8 pre-existing was captured GREEN on pristine origin/main BEFORE editing.
tests.node_absent_caveat: Host has no Node, so the mirrors re-implement the gate logic in PS-5.1 and statically parse the JS; they cannot execute orchestrator.js or embed. Real proof = the prod /api/chat test below (per the deploy-and-self-verify rule).

live_verification_prod.status: PENDING -- requires merge->main + M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP=1 on prod. To be filled with the real {response} from POSTing to https://m8-alpha.vercel.app/api/chat after deploy.
live_verification_prod.headline_before_after: BEFORE: 'tell me about my kafala operation' -> generic memory/web answer (no docs). AFTER (flag on): cited answer from his ingested docs ([KG:*]).
queries_to_post: tell me about my kafala operation   -> EXPECT: cited from his ingested docs (the kafala fix).
how much did Sara spend in June   -> EXPECT: UNCHANGED wallet answer (money lane untouched).
what was my fleet net profit on a given day   -> EXPECT: UNCHANGED fleet/finance answer.

deploy: Branch will be pushed. NOT merged. Sequence on his OK: (1) merge feat/b166-semantic-flip -> main (prod auto-deploys, atomic, ~30-60s; flag OFF so still inert = zero risk); (2) set M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP=1 in Vercel prod env (this activates the flip); (3) confirm READY; (4) I POST the queries above to the OPEN prod /api/chat and paste the real before/after. Rollback = unset M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP (instant, no redeploy) or Vercel rollback to ec317ca.
kill_switch: Unset M8_SEMANTIC_FLIP (or set !=1) -> instant revert to B-164 behaviour, no code change/redeploy needed.
